description: A minimal, clean implementation of GPT-2 training in ~300 lines of PyTorch

key_decisions.reasoning: Abstractions hide understanding. If you can read the whole thing in one sitting, you actually know what's happening.

key_decisions.decision: No framework abstractions like HuggingFace Trainer
key_decisions.reasoning: Those abstractions are great for production but terrible for learning. I wanted every line to be intentional.
Get a known baseline working first. Verify it matches expected loss. Then you have a platform to experiment from.

key_decisions.decision: Use AdamW with cosine decay + warmup
key_decisions.reasoning: This is what works. Don't reinvent the optimizer schedule, just use what the literature agrees on.

key_decisions.decision: 6 layers, 6 heads, 384 embedding dim for the small model
key_decisions.reasoning: Scaled down GPT-2 to fit on a single GPU while still being non-trivial. The architecture scales cleanly.

lessons: A clean 300-line implementation teaches more than a 10,000-line framework
Always reproduce a known result before trying to improve on it
The training loop is not magic — it's just forward, loss, backward, step
